info.title: intelli-engine v1 External API
info.description:
AI Agent Backend Platform API

提供数据源管理、自然语言查询（Text-to-SQL/ES）、报告生成等 AI 能力。

## 核心流程
1. 注册数据源 (text/file/url/sql/es)
2. 自然语言查询数据源 → 自动生成 SQL/ES DSL 并执行
3. 基于数据源生成报告 → 输出 DOCX/HTML
4. 下载报告产物

post.description:
Register a new user account (always 'user' role).

The first admin is created explicitly through /initialize. This endpoint creates regular users.
Auto-login by setting the session cookie.
Logout current user by clearing the cookie.

Change password for the currently authenticated user.

Also handles the first-boot setup flow:
- If new_email is provided, updates email (checks uniqueness)
- If user.needs_setup is True and new_email is given, clears needs_setup
- Always increments token_version to invalidate old sessions
- Re-issues session cookie with new token_version

get.description: Check if an admin account exists. Returns needs_setup=True when no admin exists.

post.description:
Create the first admin account on initial system setup.

Only callable when no admin exists. Returns 409 Conflict if an admin
already exists.

On success, the admin account is created with ``needs_setup=False`` and
the session cookie is set.

get.description:
Initiate OAuth login flow.

Redirects to the OAuth provider's authorization URL.
Currently a placeholder - requires OAuth provider implementation.

OAuth callback endpoint.

Handles the OAuth provider's callback after user authorization.
Currently a placeholder.

List all available agents.

Returns predefined system agents and any user-created custom agents.

schema.title: Response List Agents Api V1 Agents Get

get.description:
List all capabilities supported by this intelli-engine instance.

External applications can use this endpoint to discover available
features and plan their integration accordingly.

schema.title: Response Get Capabilities Api V1 Capabilities Get

post.description:
Register a new data source for a conversation.

Supports five types of data sources:

- **text**: Direct text content provided in the request body.
- **url**: A URL to be fetched (content stored as reference).
- **file**: A file previously uploaded to the conversation.
- **sql**: A SQL database connection. Metadata must include:
  - `db_type`: `mysql`, `postgresql`, `sqlite`, `mssql`, or `oracle`
  - `host`, `port`, `database`, `username`, `password`
  - Schema is auto-discovered on query (no need to provide manually).
- **es**: An Elasticsearch connection. Metadata must include:
  - `hosts`: List of ES node URLs (e.g., `["http://localhost:9200"]`)
  - `index`: Default index to query
  - `username`, `password` (optional)

get.description: List all data sources registered for a conversation.
Get a specific data source by ID.

post.description:
Query a data source using natural language.

For **SQL** data sources, converts natural language to SQL, executes it,
and returns structured results (Text-to-SQL).

For **Elasticsearch** data sources, converts natural language to ES query DSL,
executes it, and returns structured results (Text-to-ES).

For **text/file/url** data sources, returns the stored content.

### Example
```json
{
  "query": "上个月销量最高的10款产品",
  "max_results": 50
}
```

Generate a report based on data sources and conversation context.

This is an **asynchronous** endpoint. It creates a report generation job
and returns immediately with a `report_id`. Use `GET /api/v1/reports/{report_id}`
to poll for completion.

### Request body
- **title**: Report title.
- **format**: List of output formats. Supported: `docx`, `html`, `pdf`.
- **report_type**: Type of report. Options: `analysis`, `summary`, `research`, `meeting_notes`, `decision_memo`.
- **datasource_ids**: List of data source IDs to include.
- **include_conversation**: Whether to include QA from the conversation.
- **sections**: Optional custom section list. Uses defaults based on `report_type` if omitted.

get.description:
Get report generation status and download URLs.

Poll this endpoint to check the status of a report generation job.
When status is `success`, the response includes the artifact download URLs.

### Status values
- `queued`: Job is waiting to be processed.
- `processing`: Report is being generated.
- `success`: Report is ready. Check `artifacts` for download URLs.
- `failed`: Generation failed. Check `error` for details.

Download a generated report artifact.

Returns the file content with appropriate Content-Type.
Use `?download=true` to force download as attachment.

### Example
- View inline: `GET /api/v1/artifacts/art_xxx`
- Download: `GET /api/v1/artifacts/art_xxx?download=true`

SkillCategory.title: SkillCategory
SkillCategory.description:
Source category for a skill.

- ``PUBLIC``: built-in skill bundled with the platform, read-only.
- ``CUSTOM``: user-authored skill that can be edited or deleted.
